title: Digital Signatures with Ed25519 and ECDSA · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A medical device maintenance group. They need canonical versioned manifests, trusted Ed25519 signing keys, key-purpose policy, threshold approval, expiry and rollback resistance, negative vectors, offline verification, rotation, and incident revocation. Planned artifact: Deliver Signed Software Update Manifest with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.
Generate, parse, sign, and verify Ed25519 keys and signatures with exact message/context rules and trusted key selection.
Bind artifact type, version, role, tenant, key ID, expiry, and canonical bytes to prevent cross-protocol signature reuse.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Signature Guarantees
steps.instruction:
project case — A project cryptographic engineering team handles case 8471 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Signature Guarantees.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Signature Guarantees, keep Crypto Go Signature Guarantees connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
